# Scope and Core Invariants

<!-- Generated by go run ./tools/runtimeschedulingdocs -write-doc. Do not edit by hand. -->

[Back to invariants](../invariants.md)

Executable SSOT: [`runtime-scheduling.riido.json`](../../runtime-scheduling.riido.json).

Core fragment: [`core.riido.json`](core.riido.json).

This document fills the C5 Runtime Scheduling context with executable core invariants.

## Responsibilities

- Decide which runtime can claim or execute a task.
- Compare task required surfaces with runtime capability snapshots.
- Define runtime lease pinning and heartbeat meaning.

## Non-Responsibilities

- Provider static capability contracts belong to riido-contracts C3.
- Provider process execution, provider session schema, retention, and adapters belong to C4 Provider Runtime.
- Workdir creation belongs to the daemon migration workdir slice, and lock primitives belong to C9 Locking.
- Client task-thread read models and presentation copy belong to control-plane and client.

## Core Invariants

| Invariant | Rule | Evidence |
| --- | --- | --- |
| capability-only-dispatch | Schedulers branch on capability and task requirement facts, not provider binary version strings. | `capability-only-gate` |
| no-provider-specific-fsm | Scheduling compares requirement/capability envelopes; execution state is owned by the C1/C2 task FSM. | `pure-runtime-selection` |
| spawn-after-eligibility | Provider processes are spawned only after the C5 pre-submit eligibility gate accepts the task. | `pre-submit-supervisor-gate` |
| experimental-runtime-opt-in | Experimental runtimes require explicit task or assignment opt-in. | `experimental-opt-in-gate` |
| lease-runtime-pin | A runtime lease is pinned by RuntimeID plus CapabilityFingerprint; changed fingerprints make stale leases invalid. | `lease-runtime-pin` |
| file-queue-not-durable-scheduler | The local file queue is an adapter source; durable scheduling semantics belong to DB/API sources. | `file-queue-source-boundary` |
| task-db-production-source | riido-task-db.v1 claims queued rows through guarded transitions and keeps provider-completed tasks in validation. | `task-db-source-contract` |
| provider-session-outside-c5 | C5 preserves lease metadata but does not own the provider session table schema or native resume semantics. | `provider-session-boundary` |
| client-read-model-outside-c5 | C5 preserves IDs for reporting but does not own task-thread read models, active_stream selection, or client copy. | `client-read-model-boundary` |
